weather_line: Overcast in the Liaison Wing · audit weather · clearing by teatime

stories.headline: Christine Learns of the Efficiency Audit; Sources Confirm She Learned of It From the Log Itself, Which the Duty Goblin Left Open on the Corridor Lectern
body: The Gringotts Senior Audit Subcommittee's confidential review of Liaison Wing efficiency entered its second day Tuesday in a condition the Subcommittee had not anticipated: no longer confidential. The Duty Goblin, asked to maintain a discreet observational log, had by seven forty-eight placed said log on the corridor lectern in order to free both hands for his morning quill-case reorganisation. The log was open. Christine passed the lectern at seven forty-nine.

By seven fifty-one she had read all four entries, noted two factual inaccuracies, and appended a fifth entry in a different ink. The fifth entry reads: 'Tuesday. Audit log discovered. Duration of discovery: forty seconds. Inaccuracies corrected in margins. Corridor clock did not chime; it is Tuesday and the clock chimes on the quarter-hour only.' The Duty Goblin has been informed. He has placed a small marker reading 'DISPUTED' beside the fifth entry, which Christine has underlined.
The Subcommittee has been notified. It is understood that the Subcommittee is now reviewing its procedures for reviewing procedures. Christine has not been observed to find this remarkable.

stories.headline: The Kitchen Table, Having Hosted the Northeastern Chart for Six Days, Is Reported to Miss It; Steve Declines to Comment; the Table's Feelings Are Not Disputed
body: With the northeastern route resolved, the Duck Bandit identified, and the brass ducks now oriented peacefully toward the door, sources close to the kitchen table report a certain atmospheric flatness in the room that persisted through Tuesday morning. The chart has been folded and stored. The three marginal notations have been filed. Steve applied a fresh coat of sealant to the table surface, which some observers interpreted as a gesture of closure and others interpreted as routine maintenance. Steve declined to characterise it.

The brass ducks remain facing the door. The table is described by household correspondents as 'quietly available.' A new chart has not been unfolded. It is not known whether one exists. The Jeep, reached for comment via its customary channel of overnight engine-tick, issued a sound that the engineering desk assessed as 'expectant.'

stories.headline: Ministry Issues Tuesday Bulletin Confirming Tuesday; Notes That the Audit Log Incident 'Raises Questions It Is Not Prepared to Answer at This Time'; Attaches a Photograph of the Corridor Lectern, Unlabelled
body: The Ministry of Magic's Tuesday bulletin, distributed at nine o'clock by owl whose expression suggested it had read the contents, confirmed that Tuesday was occurring and described the day as 'proceeding in the expected direction.' A supplementary note addressed the Gringotts Audit Log Incident in one sentence: 'The Ministry is aware of the situation and considers the matter to be, at present, a matter.' No further elaboration was offered.

Attached to the bulletin, without caption or annotation, was a photograph of the Gringotts corridor lectern. The log is visible in the photograph, open to the page containing Christine's fifth entry. The Duty Goblin's 'DISPUTED' marker is also visible. The Ministry has not responded to questions about how it obtained the photograph, though sources note this is consistent with its approach to the duck photographs, the duck itself, and most things.

stories.headline: Warmest Lap Ruling Survives Into Tuesday; Jersey Considers This a Precedent; Legal Scholars Are Less Certain
body: Monday's warmest lap ruling, issued after twenty days of contested equilibrium and immediately sat upon, has now survived a full overnight period without formal challenge, leading Jersey to circulate what household sources describe as 'a memo of quiet satisfaction.' The memo consists of Jersey relocating to the ruled lap before the relevant party had finished their morning tea.

Lucy has not challenged the ruling directly, which legal observers note is statistically unusual and possibly strategic. Ellie, informed of the ruling's survival on Tuesday morning, received the news with evident warmth and then sat in a different chair entirely, having apparently not followed which lap was in question. Jersey has not corrected her. The ruling stands.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Studied the vacated lectern in the Gringotts corridor for purposes she declined to specify; was not present when the ink discrepancy in the audit log's third entry was noted; has no further comment at this time.

Ellie: Greeted her reflection in the corridor window with considerable warmth on Tuesday morning, found the greeting returned in full, and departed satisfied that the day had begun well; has since greeted the same window twice more as a courtesy.
Jersey: The ruling stands. Jersey is on the lap. These two facts are, in his considered assessment, the whole of Tuesday.
founding_editor_note: That lectern wants a bolt through the bottom — open face like that, anything could fall out. I'd have put a lip on it. I'll note it was not my design. — D.

in_brief.sport: Tuesday deadlift totals were not logged officially, as Christine resolved the audit situation before anyone located a parchment, which the Athletic Registry has accepted as a qualifying performance.
in_brief.forecast: Overcast through the Liaison Wing corridor; brief clearing at the lectern; warm and settled by evening, with a continued light probability of ducks.

storyline_updates.title: The Gringotts Efficiency Inquiry
storyline_updates.summary: Day 2: Christine discovered the audit via the log itself, left open on the corridor lectern by the Duty Goblin at seven forty-eight. She read it at seven forty-nine, corrected two inaccuracies, and added a fifth entry in a different ink by seven fifty-one. The Duty Goblin has placed a 'DISPUTED' marker beside the fifth entry; Christine has underlined it. The Subcommittee is now reviewing its review procedures. The Ministry has somehow obtained a photograph of the lectern. Day 3 should bring the Subcommittee into direct contact with Christine — either a formal meeting they have called, or an informal corridor encounter they did not plan — in which the audit itself begins to go in a direction the auditors had not modelled. Christine has not been observed to find any of this remarkable, which is its own data point.
